title: Caledonia 2026: What's New for Yacht Charter and Boat Rental
html:
In 2026, Caledonia offers fresh opportunities for boating enthusiasts, with new marinas and updated fleets making it easier to explore the Gulf of Porto Vecchio and beyond. Prices for motor yacht hire start from €250 for a half-day, and you can browse options on BoatHire24 to compare verified boat listings.

Where to set off in Caledonia

Begin your journey from Port de Plaisance in Ajaccio, a well-equipped marina in the capital. Further south, Propriano offers easy access to the Gulf of Valinco. For those exploring the east coast, Port de Bastia provides a gateway to the islands of Cap Corse. The secluded bay of Roccapina is ideal for anchoring, while the Lavezzi Islands offer pristine waters and sandy beaches.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size and intended activities. The following options are popular for 2026 charters.

  1. Motor yacht, Best for speed and luxury on island hops, up to 10 guests, from €800 half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran, Ideal for stable family cruising, up to 12 guests, from €1,200 full-day.
  3. RIB (rigid inflatable), Perfect for coastal exploration and water sports, up to 8 guests, from €300 half-day.
  4. Sunseeker 65, A sleek flybridge for entertaining, up to 8 guests, from €1,500 full-day.
  5. Small runabout, Great for couples or small groups seeking freedom, up to 4 guests, from €200 half-day.

Before booking, consult the Mefasa 90 listing for a detailed view of a premium motor yacht option.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Caledonia?

Prices for boat rental in Caledonia vary widely depending on boat type and duration, starting from €80 per person for a shared tour.

  • Shared group tour (half-day, per person): €80, €150.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): €250, €400.
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): €1,200, €2,000.
  • Full-crewed yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): €2,000, €3,500.

To find the best deals, consider visiting yacht charter Caledonia listings on BoatHire24 for transparent pricing and availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Caledonia?

Boat licence requirements in Caledonia follow French regulations, and the operator will confirm exact prerequisites upon booking.

For boats with an engine power above 6 kW (approx. 8 hp), a valid boating licence is generally required. Rental companies often accept an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a national equivalent. Some smaller boats may be rented without a licence if the operator deems it suitable after a brief briefing.

For a quieter experience, head to the bay of Cupabia in the morning before the wind picks up; the shallow waters are perfect for a relaxed swim.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80, €3,500 per day
  • Peak months: July, August, September
  • Trip length: Half-day to full-day charters
  • Maximum capacity: Up to 12 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Caledonia

Can I rent a boat without experience?

Yes, many operators offer skippered charters so you can enjoy the scenery without needing any boating experience.

What is the best time of year for boating in Caledonia?

The ideal window is from May to October, when the weather is warm and the Mistral wind is less frequent.

Are there any hidden fees I should know about?

Always check whether fuel, port fees, and VAT are included in the quoted price; most listings on BoatHire24 specify these details.

How far is it from Ajaccio to the Lavezzi Islands?

The distance is roughly 55 nautical miles, making it a full-day adventure for a motor yacht.

Whether you are a seasoned sailor or a first-time explorer, renting a boat in Caledonia in 2026 is straightforward with platforms like BoatHire24. The company is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat or list your own. As the destination evolves, you can find both new vessels and traditional charters suited to every need.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Caledonia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
